title: Houses for Rent in St. Clair-Superior, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in St. Clair-Superior, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Find houses for rent in St. Clair-Superior (44103), Cleveland. Rent ranges, transit, and Section 8-friendly homes from a local rental team. Book a showing.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent St. Clair-Superior cleveland
answerBox: St. Clair-Superior sits on Cleveland's near-east side in ZIP code 44103, just north of downtown and home to the AsiaTown business district. Two- and three-bedroom rental homes here typically run from about $750 to $1,300 a month, and Rent Finder Cleveland works with Section 8-friendly homes in and around the neighborhood.

sections.h2: Renting a house in St. Clair-Superior
body: St. Clair-Superior sits on Cleveland's near-east side, just north of downtown, in **ZIP code 44103**, and takes its name from the two parallel avenues — St. Clair Avenue and Superior Avenue — that run through it. The neighborhood is also home to **AsiaTown**, a business district of restaurants and shops along Payne Avenue. Housing stock is a mix of early-1900s two- and three-family homes, brick duplexes, and smaller single-family houses, many built close to the street on narrow lots typical of Cleveland's older near-east-side blocks.

The neighborhood's proximity to downtown and the Campus District makes it a common option for renters who want a shorter commute without downtown apartment pricing. Many blocks mix owner-occupied homes with rentals, and porches, small front yards, and detached garages are common features on the older housing stock here.

We work with Section 8-friendly rental homes in and around St. Clair-Superior, including the adjoining [Hough](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-hough-cleveland/) area. [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see what's currently available, or browse our broader [Cleveland houses for rent](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) listings.

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent in St. Clair-Superior?
body: Two-bedroom homes in the 44103 area typically rent from about $750 to $1,050 a month, and three-bedroom homes commonly run $1,000 to $1,350, depending on size, condition, and whether utilities are included. These figures sit within the broader $700–$1,800 range across the East Side homes Rent Finder Cleveland works with, where the typical rent is close to $1,000. Citywide, Cleveland's median asking rent was $1,250 a month as of July 2026, per Zumper — St. Clair-Superior's near-east-side rental stock tends to price at or below that citywide figure.

rows: Often a duplex unit with a shared yard

body: St. Clair-Superior is well served by GCRTA bus routes and sits close to the highways that connect downtown to the rest of the East Side.

items: **ZIP code:** 44103, shared with parts of the Hough neighborhood

**Transit:** GCRTA bus routes along St. Clair Avenue and Superior Avenue; the HealthLine bus-rapid-transit runs along Euclid Avenue just south of the neighborhood, connecting Public Square to University Circle
**Highways:** Close to I-90 and I-77 for driving downtown or to the East Side suburbs
**Local landmarks:** The AsiaTown business district along Payne Avenue; proximity to Downtown Cleveland and the Campus District

sections.h2: Section 8 and vouchers in St. Clair-Superior
body: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with — including our St. Clair-Superior-area rentals — accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) administers the voucher program locally and accepts [preliminary applications year-round](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php). Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and the City of Cleveland itself does not have a source-of-income protection ordinance — so always confirm directly with any specific landlord. Learn more in our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/).

sections.h2: Touring and applying for a home in St. Clair-Superior
body: Once you find a St. Clair-Superior-area listing you're interested in, the process is straightforward: book a free, no-obligation showing to walk through the home in person, then start your rental application online through our secure DoorLoop portal when you're ready. We're happy to answer questions about a specific property — including whether a voucher works for it — before you commit to a showing.

Because St. Clair-Superior sits close to downtown and the Campus District, renters relocating for work near Public Square or Cleveland State University often shortlist this neighborhood alongside nearby Hough and the Warehouse District.

St. Clair-Superior borders [Hough](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-hough-cleveland/) to the southeast and is a short drive from [Glenville](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-glenville-cleveland/) near University Circle. If you're flexible on the exact neighborhood, comparing nearby East Side options can widen the pool of available homes.

faq.q: What ZIP code is St. Clair-Superior in?
faq.a: St. Clair-Superior is located in ZIP code 44103 on Cleveland's near-east side, just north of downtown. The neighborhood includes the AsiaTown business district and borders the Hough neighborhood to the southeast.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in St. Clair-Superior?
faq.a: Yes. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with, including rentals in and around St. Clair-Superior, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Book a free showing to see current availability.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in St. Clair-Superior?
faq.a: Two-bedroom homes typically rent from about $750 to $1,050 a month, and three-bedroom homes run roughly $1,000 to $1,350, depending on size and condition.

faq.q: How do I get around St. Clair-Superior without a car?
faq.a: Several GCRTA bus routes run along St. Clair and Superior Avenues, and the HealthLine bus-rapid-transit line runs along Euclid Avenue just south of the neighborhood, connecting to Public Square and University Circle.

faq.q: Does Cleveland protect Section 8 voucher holders from discrimination?
faq.a: Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law, and the City of Cleveland does not currently have its own ordinance protecting voucher holders. Some suburbs, including Cleveland Heights and South Euclid, do have local protections. Check the current rules for any specific city before you sign a lease.

faq.q: How do I apply for a house in St. Clair-Superior?
faq.a: Start by booking a free showing to see the home in person. If it's a fit, you can submit your rental application online through our secure DoorLoop portal. We can also answer questions about a specific property, including whether it works with a Housing Choice Voucher, before you schedule a tour.
